page: Vultr vx1-m-192c-1536g-11520s is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 192 shared AMD vCPUs and 1430.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 7.45 GB of RAM per core. The instance is equipped with a substantial local storage allocation of 10720 GB on NVMe SSDs, and includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this server type.

The server utilizes a shared CPU allocation model despite its large scale of 192 vCPUs. This shared resource structure represents a tradeoff, as CPU performance may vary depending on co-tenant activity, making it highly cost-efficient for resource-dense workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated single-core execution. The massive 10720 GB NVMe SSD storage array provides high-capacity local storage directly attached to the instance, supporting intensive data-retrieval operations.

Given the high memory capacity of 1430.0 GB and extensive NVMe storage, this server is configured for large-scale database hosting, big data processing, memory-intensive analytics, and storage-heavy workloads. The combination of high memory density and local NVMe storage supports complex data pipelines and distributed caching systems that can tolerate shared CPU allocation.
description: Vultr vx1-m-192c-1536g-11520s is a high-capacity x86_64 server within the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 192 shared AMD vCPUs and 1430.0 GB of RAM. This configuration provides approximately 7.45 GB of memory per core, paired with a massive 10720 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address and lacks GPU acceleration. Operating on a shared CPU allocation model, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for resource-dense workloads where absolute CPU dedication is secondary to massive memory and storage capacity. It is well-suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, distributed caching, and memory-intensive analytical workloads that benefit from extensive local NVMe storage.
